autoDeploy.title: Auto-Deploy
autoDeploy.description: Enable auto-deploy to trigger deployments automatically on every push to your main branch.

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.description: Import a Git repository or use a template to create your first deployment.

card.activeTitle: This is the deployment currently serving the project
card.pinnedTitle: Pinned — exempt from retention prune. Stays rollback-restorable indefinitely.
card.snapshottedTitle: Artifact archived — this version is available to roll back to

filtered.title: No deployments found
filtered.description: Try adjusting your filters or clearing your search.

none.title: No deployments yet
none.description: Deploy your first project and it will appear here with build status, commit details, and performance metrics.

manual.title: Manual Deployment
manual.description: This deployment was triggered manually and doesn't have commit information.

rollbackTitle.enabled: Restore the project to this version (code only — data is unchanged)
rollbackTitle.active: This is already the active version
rollbackTitle.pruned: Rollback artifact has been pruned. Use 'Redeploy this commit' to rebuild from source instead.
rollbackTitle.notReady: Only ready deployments can be rolled back to

menu.redeployTitle: Rebuild this exact commit from source. Slower than rollback but works after the artifact has been pruned.

pinTitle.pin: Keep this deployment rollback-restorable indefinitely (cap: 10 pinned per project)
pinTitle.unpin: Allow this deployment to be pruned when retention overflows
pinTitle.pruned: Cannot pin — artifact has already been pruned

menu.confirmRollback:
Roll back to this version?

This restores the application code only. Database and volume data are unchanged.
menu.confirmRedeploy:
Redeploy commit {hash}?

Rebuilds this exact commit from source. Slower than rollback (full build runs again), but works even after the rollback artifact has been pruned. A new deployment will appear in the list.
